message:
test(resolv): smoke-fuzz proptests for parse_services_line (bd-s170)

Three smoke-fuzz proptests for /etc/services parsing:

fuzz_parse_services_line_never_panics
  Arbitrary 0..512-byte sequences. Must never panic.

fuzz_parse_services_line_invariants
  When Some(entry) is returned, asserts:
    • entry.name and entry.protocol are both non-empty
    • entry.name, entry.protocol, and every alias are substrings of
      the pre-'#'-comment input region
  Catches synthesis of phantom data and under/over-strip of the
  comment boundary.

fuzz_parse_services_line_structured_alphabet_never_panics
  Biased over {space, tab, '#', '/', 0-9, a-z, A-Z, '-', '_', '.',
  0x00, 0xff}. The '/' delimiter separates port from protocol —
  random bytes almost never produce one in the right position, so
  this strategy is what actually exercises the port u16 parse path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
